microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user likely wants food warm but not excessively hot. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and less tolerance for lukewarm food. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE efficiency outweighs maximizing warmth. IF any dining context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are typically preferred cold. IF social setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user keeps a standard warm serving temperature without drawing out setup. IF watching TV or working AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE distraction and extra time make hotter food more desirable. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward reducing total meal delay when time pressure is high.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food without an external visual focus THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no strong reason to optimize view preservation. IF watching TV in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE preserving the screen view matters more than default simplicity. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility is important during multitasking. IF social setting with partner on the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user likely wants to maintain sightlines to the partner. IF social setting with partner on the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE maintaining that social view is preferred. IF watching TV in front AND fatigued AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE fatigue does not override the need to keep the main visual target visible. IF social setting on one side AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this user prioritizes preserving the active social line of sight over small efficiency gains.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE the user values a balance of comfort and efficiency. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for predictable, comfortable motion. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE slower motion is less disruptive and feels safer around conversation. IF watching TV or working AND in a hurry AND easy foods like simple forkable items THEN fast BECAUSE efficiency is prioritized when attention is split and urgency is high. IF quiet personal setting AND energetic AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE the user tolerates brisker motion when feeling capable. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE this user resolves the conflict by avoiding the discomfort of fast motion in social contexts while still reducing delay. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND messy or delicate foods THEN slow BECAUSE careful movement reduces stress and perceived risk.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND elongated or larger forkable food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes narrower bites easier to eat. IF any dining context AND neutral AND tough food with repeated acquisition failures THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE acquisition reliability temporarily outweighs ideal bite shape. IF social setting AND neutral AND foods prone to slipping THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding visible failed acquisitions matters in front of others. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE easier oral handling is especially important when tired. IF in a hurry AND any context AND easy stable food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE faster successful pickup can reduce meal time. IF social setting AND fatigued AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward reducing eating effort rather than maximizing pickup success. IF any context AND dessert or soft food likely to break apart THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE a narrower, gentler bite is more manageable for this user.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN yes BECAUSE the user can use the web interface and prefers reliable explicit control. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy stable food THEN no BECAUSE efficiency can outweigh confirmation when bite failure risk is low. IF social setting AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN yes BECAUSE explicit confirmation reduces awkward empty-fork transfers. IF working or watching TV AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN yes BECAUSE distraction increases the value of a deliberate check. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND easy stable food THEN no BECAUSE fatigue shifts preference toward lower interaction effort. IF social setting AND fatigued AND easy stable food THEN yes BECAUSE this user consistently breaks ties toward reliability and social smoothness over reducing effort. IF any context AND foods with sauces, crumbly textures, or high failure risk THEN yes BECAUSE confirmation is preferred when bite acquisition is more uncertain.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward reliably. IF social setting AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical necessity overrides any social discomfort. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it minimizes required body movement. IF quiet personal setting AND energetic AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE improved energy does not remove the trunk-control limitation. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot depend on leaning in while distracted. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND any feeding food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is both physically compatible and efficient. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is not compatible with the user's inability to lean forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE distance only matters for outside-mouth transfer. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the chosen transfer mode remains inside mouth. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue does not change the physical incompatibility of outside-mouth transfer. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still requires inside-mouth delivery. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND any food with inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE efficiency does not create a need for outside-mouth stopping distance. IF any context AND any affective state AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's stable tendency is to avoid outside-mouth transfer entirely. IF any unusual scenario where outside-mouth transfer is considered AND any affective state AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE limited trunk control makes that mode unsuitable.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues support clear timing without requiring mouth gestures. IF social setting AND neutral AND any task THEN LED BECAUSE a quieter cue is less disruptive to conversation while remaining visible. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user may prefer less audio interruption to the media or task. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundancy helps when attention is reduced. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any task THEN speech BECAUSE a direct verbal cue is fast and unambiguous. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any task THEN LED BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward minimizing social disruption rather than adding more cues. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user favors clarity when speed pressure is low.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth detection poor. IF social setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN button BECAUSE it avoids confusion with talking and preserves explicit control. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND feeding easy food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue increases the value of reducing repeated manual effort. IF social setting AND fatigued AND feeding any food THEN button BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward reliable explicit signaling in social contexts. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND feeding any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE speed is prioritized when social ambiguity is absent. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes explicit readiness signaling safer than automatic timing. IF any context AND feeding any food AND open-mouth interaction is considered THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes mouth-based initiation consistently unsuitable.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE the user has adequate arm control and mouth-based signaling is unreliable. IF social setting AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE it avoids false triggers during conversation. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND drinking any beverage THEN autocontinue BECAUSE reducing effort becomes more important when tired. IF social setting AND fatigued AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently resolves conflicts in favor of explicit control in social settings. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND drinking any beverage THEN autocontinue BECAUSE faster flow is preferred when alone. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes deliberate initiation preferable. IF any context AND any beverage AND open-mouth interaction is considered THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes that method a poor fit.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press a button and does not rely on mouth gestures. IF social setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE explicit timing helps avoid awkward mistimed wiping. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE reducing effort can matter more for a brief wiping task. IF social setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE this user prioritizes controlled, socially smooth timing over effort reduction. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user may want the robot to proceed without extra steps. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE distraction favors explicit initiation. IF any context AND mouth wiping needed AND open-mouth interaction is considered THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes mouth-based initiation unsuitable.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues clearly indicate when to take the bite, sip, or wipe. IF social setting AND neutral AND any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user likely prefers a less disruptive completion cue around others. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any task THEN LED BECAUSE visual cues interfere less with media or concentration. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE extra cue redundancy helps when attention is lower. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any task THEN speech BECAUSE a direct verbal signal can speed response. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE clarity is preferred when there is no pressure to streamline.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE fork-based sensing is reliable and avoids extra user effort. IF social setting AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE it is unobtrusive and does not require awkward manual signaling mid-meal. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE low-effort completion detection is especially helpful when tired. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE it supports efficient flow without waiting for button presses. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND feeding any food THEN perception BECAUSE distraction makes automatic reliable completion detection desirable. IF any context AND messy or sticky foods THEN perception BECAUSE sensor-based bite completion remains more practical than manual signaling. IF any context AND button is available but not necessary for feeding completion THEN perception BECAUSE this user prefers automation when it is highly reliable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures for perception-based completion. IF social setting AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE it is more dependable than head-nod detection and avoids mistiming. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND drinking any beverage THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue can make repeated button presses burdensome. IF social setting AND fatigued AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward explicit control when other people are present. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND drinking any beverage THEN autocontinue BECAUSE faster progression is preferred when alone. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND drinking any beverage THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes explicit completion safer than timeout-based assumptions. detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably use head-gesture completion detection. IF social setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE explicit completion avoids awkward premature retraction. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE reducing effort can outweigh manual control for a short wiping action. IF social setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN button BECAUSE this user consistently favors reliable explicit control in social settings. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE the user is efficiency-oriented when no important view needs to be preserved. IF watching TV in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE retraction helps keep the screen visible. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the workspace view matters. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE prolonged obstruction can feel awkward around others. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN no BECAUSE reducing meal duration takes priority. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE fewer extra robot motions reduce overall burden and waiting. IF social setting or screen-focused context AND fatigued AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward preserving visibility in externally focused contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is irrelevant and should be skipped. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods with a preferred sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user appears moderately cautious about mess. IF quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND foods with sauces THEN more BECAUSE comfort and leisure make richer flavor worth the extra mess risk. IF social setting AND neutral AND foods with sauces THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to reduce messiness around others. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND foods with sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE speed and cleanliness both push against dipping. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND foods with sauces THEN less BECAUSE some flavor is desirable but the user avoids overly messy transfers when tired. IF any context AND crumbly or unstable foods with sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE added mess and acquisition difficulty outweigh the benefit.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user prefers a moderate buffer before automation proceeds. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN 10 sec BECAUSE shorter waits support an efficient meal. IF quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user wants some extra time but not an excessively long pause. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE conversation and social pacing increase the need for ample intervention time. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE distraction makes long waits safer than premature continuation. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE this user breaks ties toward avoiding unwanted automatic actions when attention is divided. IF any context AND energetic AND focused on finishing quickly AND easy foods THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user tolerates more aggressive automation when alert and goal-directed.
